name: streamdeck

metadata.name: streamdeck
metadata.summary: Library to control Elgato StreamDeck devices.

documentation:
# Python Elgato Stream Deck Library

![Example Deck](ExampleDeck.jpg)

This is an open source Python 3 library to control an
[Elgato Stream Deck](https://www.elgato.com/en/gaming/stream-deck) directly,
without the official software. This can allow you to create your own custom
front-ends, such as a custom control front-end for home automation software.

_________________

[PyPi Project Entry](https://pypi.org/project/streamdeck/) - [Online Documentation](https://python-elgato-streamdeck.readthedocs.io) - [Source Code](https://github.com/abcminiuser/python-elgato-streamdeck)


## Project Status:

Working - you can enumerate devices, set the brightness of the panel(s), set
the images shown on each button, and read the current button states.

Currently the following StreamDeck products are supported in multiple hardware
variants:

* StreamDeck Mini
* StreamDeck Neo
* StreamDeck Original
* StreamDeck Pedal
* StreamDeck Plus
* StreamDeck XL

## Package Installation:

Install the library via pip:

```
pip install streamdeck
```

Alternatively, manually clone the project repository:

```
git clone https://github.com/abcminiuser/python-elgato-streamdeck.git
```

For detailed installation instructions, refer to the prebuilt
[online documentation](https://python-elgato-streamdeck.readthedocs.io), or
build the documentation yourself locally by running `make html` from the `docs`
directory.


## Credits:

I've used the reverse engineering notes from
[this GitHub](https://github.com/alvancamp/node-elgato-stream-deck/blob/master/NOTES.md)
repository to implement this library. Thanks Alex Van Camp!

Thank you to the following contributors, large and small, for helping with the
development and maintenance of this library:

- [admiral0](https://github.com/admiral0)
- [Aetherdyne](https://github.com/Aetherdyne)
- [benedikt-bartscher](https://github.com/benedikt-bartscher)
- [brimston3](https://github.com/brimston3)

- [Core447](https://github.com/Core447)
- [dirkk0](https://github.com/dirkk0)
- [dodgyrabbit](https://github.com/dodgyrabbit)
- [dubstech](https://github.com/dubstech)
- [Giraut](https://github.com/Giraut)
- [impala454](https://github.com/impala454)
- [itsusony](https://github.com/itsusony)
- [jakobbuis](https://github.com/jakobbuis)
- [jmudge14](https://github.com/jmudge14)
- [Kalle-Wirsch](https://github.com/Kalle-Wirsch)
- [karstlok](https://github.com/karstlok)
- [Lewiscowles1986](https://github.com/Lewiscowles1986)
- [m-weigand](https://github.com/m-weigand)
- [matrixinius](https://github.com/matrixinius)
- [phillco](https://github.com/phillco)
- [pointshader](https://github.com/pointshader)
- [shanna](https://github.com/shanna)
- [spidererrol](https://github.com/Spidererrol)
- [spyoungtech](https://github.com/spyoungtech)

If you've contributed in some manner, but I've accidentally missed you in the
list above, please let me know.

classes.name: StreamDeck.DeviceManager.ProbeError
documentation:
Exception thrown when attempting to probe for attached StreamDeck devices,
but no suitable valid transport was found.

classes.name: StreamDeck.DeviceManager.DeviceManager

methods.name: StreamDeck.DeviceManager.DeviceManager._get_transport
documentation:
Creates a new HID transport instance from the given transport back-end
name. If no specific transport is supplied, an attempt to find an
installed backend will be made.

```
param str transport

Name of a supported HID transport back-end to use, None to autoprobe.

rtype

Transport.* instance

return

Instance of a HID Transport class
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.DeviceManager.DeviceManager.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new StreamDeck DeviceManager, used to detect attached StreamDeck devices.

:param str transport: name of the the specific HID transport back-end to use, None to auto-probe.

methods.name: StreamDeck.DeviceManager.DeviceManager.enumerate
documentation:
Detect attached StreamDeck devices.

```
rtype

list(StreamDeck)

return

list of  instances, one for each detected device.
```

Central device manager, to enumerate any attached StreamDeck devices. An
instance of this class must be created in order to detect and use any
StreamDeck devices.

classes.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.TransportError
documentation:
Exception thrown when attempting to access a device using a backend
transport that has failed (for example, if the requested device could not
be accessed).

classes.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.probe
documentation:
Attempts to determine if the back-end is installed and usable. It is
expected that probe failures throw exceptions detailing their exact
cause of failure.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.enumerate
documentation:
Enumerates all available devices on the system using the current
transport back-end.

```
param int vid

USB Vendor ID to filter all devices by,  if the
device list should not be filtered by vendor.

param int pid

USB Product ID to filter all devices by,  if the
device list should not be filtered by product.

rtype

list(Transport.Device)

return

List of discovered devices that are available through this
transport back-end.
```

inner_classes.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.open
documentation:
Opens the device for input/output. This must be called prior to
sending or receiving any reports.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~Transport.Device.close` for the
corresponding close method.
:::

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.close
documentation:
Closes the device for input/output.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~~Transport.Device.open` for the
corresponding open method.
:::

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.is_open
documentation:
Indicates if the physical device object this instance is attached
to has been opened by the host.

```
rtype

bool

return

 if the device is open,  otherwise.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.connected
documentation:
Indicates if the physical device object this instance is attached
to is still connected to the host.

```
rtype

bool

return

 if the device is still connected,  otherwise.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.path
documentation:
Retrieves the logical path of the attached device within the
current system. This can be used to uniquely differentiate one
device from another.

```
rtype

str

return

Logical device path for the attached device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.vendor_id
documentation:
Retrieves the vendor ID value of the attached device.

```
rtype

int

return

Vendor ID of the attached device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.product_id
documentation:
Retrieves the product ID value of the attached device.

```
rtype

int

return

Product ID of the attached device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.write_feature
documentation:
Sends a HID Feature report to the open HID device.

```
param enumerable() payload

Enumerate list of bytes to send to the
device, as a feature report. The first
byte of the report should be the Report
ID of the report being sent.

rtype

int

return

Number of bytes successfully sent to the device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.read_feature
documentation:
Reads a HID Feature report from the open HID device.

```
param int report_id

Report ID of the report being read.

param int length

Maximum length of the Feature report to read.

rtype

list(byte)

return

List of bytes containing the read Feature report. The
first byte of the report will be the Report ID of the
report that was read.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.write
documentation:
Sends a HID Out report to the open HID device.

```
param enumerable() payload

Enumerate list of bytes to send to the
device, as an Out report. The first
byte of the report should be the Report
ID of the report being sent.

rtype

int

return

Number of bytes successfully sent to the device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.Transport.Transport.Device.read
documentation:
Performs a blocking read of a HID In report from the open HID device.

```
param int length

Maximum length of the In report to read.

rtype

list(byte)

return

List of bytes containing the read In report. The first byte
of the report will be the Report ID of the report that was
read.
```

Base connection device, representing an abstract connected device which
can be communicated with by an upper layer high level protocol.

Base transport layer, representing an abstract communication back-end which
can be used to discovery attached StreamDeck devices.

modules.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper
documentation: Python Stream Deck Library
Released under the MIT license
dean [at] fourwalledcubicle [dot] com
www.fourwalledcubicle.com

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.create_image
documentation:
:::{deprecated} 0.9.5 Use :func:`~PILHelper.create_key_image` method instead.
:::

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.create_key_image
documentation:
Creates a new PIL Image with the correct image dimensions for the given
StreamDeck device's keys.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.to_native_key_format` method for converting a
PIL image instance to the native key image format of a given
StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible image for.

param str background

Background color to use, compatible with .

rtype

PIL.Image

return

Created PIL image
```

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.create_touchscreen_image
documentation:
Creates a new PIL Image with the correct image dimensions for the given
StreamDeck device's touchscreen.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.to_native_touchscreen_format` method for converting a
PIL image instance to the native touchscreen image format of a given
StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible image for.

param str background

Background color to use, compatible with .

rtype

PIL.Image

return

Created PIL image
```

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.create_screen_image
documentation:
Creates a new PIL Image with the correct image dimensions for the given
StreamDeck device's creen.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.to_native_screen_format` method for converting a
PIL image instance to the native screen image format of a given
StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible image for.

param str background

Background color to use, compatible with .

rtype

PIL.Image

return

Created PIL image
```

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.create_scaled_image
documentation:
:::{deprecated} 0.9.5 Use :func:`~PILHelper.create_scaled_key_image` method instead.
:::

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.create_scaled_key_image
documentation:
Creates a new key image that contains a scaled version of a given image,
resized to best fit the given StreamDeck device's keys with the given
margins around each side.

The scaled image is centered within the new key image, offset by the given
margins. The aspect ratio of the image is preserved.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.to_native_key_format` method for converting a
PIL image instance to the native key image format of a given
StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible image for.

param Image image

PIL Image object to scale

param list(int)

Array of margin pixels in (top, right, bottom, left) order.

param str background

Background color to use, compatible with .

rtrype

PIL.Image

return

Loaded PIL image scaled and centered
```

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.create_scaled_touchscreen_image
documentation:
Creates a new touchscreen image that contains a scaled version of a given image,
resized to best fit the given StreamDeck device's touchscreen with the given
margins around each side.

The scaled image is centered within the new touchscreen image, offset by the given
margins. The aspect ratio of the image is preserved.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.to_native_touchscreen_format` method for converting a
PIL image instance to the native key image format of a given
StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible image for.

param Image image

PIL Image object to scale

param list(int)

Array of margin pixels in (top, right, bottom, left) order.

param str background

Background color to use, compatible with .

rtrype

PIL.Image

return

Loaded PIL image scaled and centered
```

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.create_scaled_screen_image
documentation:
Creates a new screen image that contains a scaled version of a given image,
resized to best fit the given StreamDeck device's screen with the given
margins around each side.

The scaled image is centered within the new screen image, offset by the given
margins. The aspect ratio of the image is preserved.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.to_native_screen_format` method for converting a
PIL image instance to the native key image format of a given
StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible image for.

param Image image

PIL Image object to scale

param list(int)

Array of margin pixels in (top, right, bottom, left) order.

param str background

Background color to use, compatible with .

rtrype

PIL.Image

return

Loaded PIL image scaled and centered
```

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.to_native_format
documentation:
:::{deprecated} 0.9.5 Use :func:`~PILHelper.to_native_key_format` method instead.
:::

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.to_native_key_format
documentation:
Converts a given PIL image to the native key image format for a StreamDeck,
suitable for passing to {func}`~StreamDeck.set_key_image`.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.create_image` method for creating a PIL
image instance for a given StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible native image for.

param PIL.Image image

PIL Image to convert to the native StreamDeck image format

rtype

enumerable()

return

Image converted to the given StreamDeck's native format
```

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.to_native_touchscreen_format
documentation:
Converts a given PIL image to the native touchscreen image format for a StreamDeck,
suitable for passing to {func}`~StreamDeck.set_touchscreen_image`.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.create_touchscreen_image` method for creating a PIL
image instance for a given StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible native image for.

param PIL.Image image

PIL Image to convert to the native StreamDeck image format

rtype

enumerable()

return

Image converted to the given StreamDeck's native touchscreen format
```

functions.name: StreamDeck.ImageHelpers.PILHelper.to_native_screen_format
documentation:
Converts a given PIL image to the native screen image format for a StreamDeck,
suitable for passing to {func}`~StreamDeck.set_screen_image`.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~PILHelper.create_screen_image` method for creating a PIL
image instance for a given StreamDeck device.
:::

```
param StreamDeck deck

StreamDeck device to generate a compatible native image for.

param PIL.Image image

PIL Image to convert to the native StreamDeck image format

rtype

enumerable()

return

Image converted to the given StreamDeck's native screen format
```

classes.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library._load_hidapi_library
documentation:
Loads the given LibUSB HIDAPI dynamic library from the host system,
if available.

```
rtype

ctypes.CDLL

return

Loaded HIDAPI library instance, or None if no library was found.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new LibUSB HIDAPI library instance, used to interface with
HID devices attached to the host system.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library.enumerate
documentation:
Enumerates all available USB HID devices on the system.

```
param int vid

USB Vendor ID to filter all devices by,  if the
device list should not be filtered by vendor.

param int pid

USB Product ID to filter all devices by,  if the
device list should not be filtered by product.

rtype

list(dict())

return

List of discovered USB HID device attributes.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library.open_device
documentation:
Opens a HID device by its canonical path on the host system.

```
rtype

Handle

return

Device handle if opened successfully, None if open failed.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library.close_device
documentation:
Closes a HID device by its open device handle on the host system.

:param Handle handle: Device handle to close.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library.send_feature_report
documentation:
Sends a HID Feature report to an open HID device.

```
param Handle handle

Device handle to access.

param bytearray() data

Array of bytes to send to the device, as a
feature report. The first byte of the
report should be the Report ID of the
report being sent.

rtype

int

return

Number of bytes successfully sent to the device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library.get_feature_report
documentation:
Retrieves a HID Feature report from an open HID device.

```
param Handle handle

Device handle to access.

param int report_id

Report ID of the report being read.

param int length

Maximum length of the Feature report to read.

rtype

bytearray()

return

Array of bytes containing the read Feature report. The
first byte of the report will be the Report ID of the
report that was read.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library.write
documentation:
Writes a HID Out report to an open HID device.

```
param Handle handle

Device handle to access.

param bytearray() data

Array of bytes to send to the device, as an
out report. The first byte of the report
should be the Report ID of the report being
sent.

rtype

int

return

Number of bytes successfully sent to the device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Transport.LibUSBHIDAPI.LibUSBHIDAPI.Library.read
documentation:
Performs a non-blocking read of a HID In report from an open HID device.

```
param Handle handle

Device handle to access.

param int length

Maximum length of the In report to read.

rtype

bytearray()

return

Array of bytes containing the read In report. The
first byte of the report will be the Report ID of the
report that was read.
```

USB HID transport layer, using the LibUSB HIDAPI dynamically linked library
directly via ctypes.

modules.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck
documentation: Python Stream Deck Library
Released under the MIT license
dean [at] fourwalledcubicle [dot] com
www.fourwalledcubicle.com

classes.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.TouchscreenEventType
documentation: Type of event that has occurred for a Touchscreen.

classes.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.DialEventType
documentation: Type of event that has occurred for a Dial.

classes.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.ControlType
documentation:
Type of control. This is used when returning internal events from a
StreamDeck subclass.

:meta private:

classes.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.__del__
documentation:
Delete handler for the StreamDeck, automatically closing the transport
if it is currently open and terminating the transport reader thread.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.__enter__
documentation:
Enter handler for the StreamDeck, taking the exclusive update lock on
the deck. This can be used in a `with` statement to ensure that only one
thread is currently updating the deck, even if it is doing multiple
operations (e.g. setting the image on multiple keys).

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.__exit__
documentation:
Exit handler for the StreamDeck, releasing the exclusive update lock on
the deck.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck._read_control_states
documentation:
Reads the raw key states from an attached StreamDeck.

```
return

dictionary containing states for all controls
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck._reset_key_stream
documentation:
Sends a blank key report to the StreamDeck, resetting the key image
streamer in the device. This prevents previously started partial key
writes that were not completed from corrupting images sent from this
application.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck._extract_string
documentation:
Extracts out a human-readable string from a collection of raw bytes,
removing any trailing whitespace or data after the first NUL byte.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck._read
documentation:
Read handler for the underlying transport, listening for control state
changes on the underlying device, caching the new states and firing off
any registered callbacks.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck._setup_reader
documentation:
Sets up the internal transport reader thread with the given callback,
for asynchronous processing of HID events from the device. If the thread
already exists, it is terminated and restarted with the new callback
function.

:param function callback: Callback to run on the reader thread.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.open
documentation:
Opens the device for input/output. This must be called prior to setting
or retrieving any device state.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.close` for the corresponding close method.
:::

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.close
documentation:
Closes the device for input/output.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.open` for the corresponding open method.
:::

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.is_open
documentation:
Indicates if the StreamDeck device is currently open and ready for use.

```
rtype

bool

return

 if the deck is open,  otherwise.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.connected
documentation:
Indicates if the physical StreamDeck device this instance is attached to
is still connected to the host.

```
rtype

bool

return

 if the deck is still connected,  otherwise.
```

Retrieves the vendor ID attached StreamDeck. This can be used
to determine the exact type of attached StreamDeck.

```
rtype

int

return

Vendor ID of the attached device.
```

documentation:
Retrieves the product ID attached StreamDeck. This can be used
to determine the exact type of attached StreamDeck.

```
rtype

int

return

Product ID of the attached device.
```

documentation:
Retrieves the physical ID of the attached StreamDeck. This can be used
to differentiate one StreamDeck from another.

```
rtype

str

return

Identifier for the attached device.
```

```
rtype

int

return

Number of physical buttons.
```

int

Retrieves number of physical dials on the attached StreamDeck device.

int

Number of physical dials
```

documentation:
Retrieves the model of Stream Deck.

```
rtype

str

String containing the model name of the StreamDeck device.

documentation:
Returns whether the Stream Deck has a visual display output.

```
rtype

bool

return

 if the deck has a screen,  otherwise.
```

Returns whether the Stream Deck can receive touch events

```
rtype

bool

return

 if the deck can receive touch events,  otherwise
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.key_layout
documentation:
Retrieves the physical button layout on the attached StreamDeck device.

```
rtype

(int, int)

return (rows, columns)

Number of button rows and columns.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.key_image_format
documentation:
Retrieves the image format accepted by the attached StreamDeck device.
Images should be given in this format when setting an image on a button.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.set_key_image` method to update the
image displayed on a StreamDeck button.
:::

```
rtype

dict()

return

Dictionary describing the various image parameters
(size, image format, image mirroring and rotation).
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.touchscreen_image_format
documentation:
Retrieves the image format accepted by the touchscreen of the Stream
Deck. Images should be given in this format when drawing on
touchscreen.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.set_touchscreen_image` method to
draw an image on the StreamDeck touchscreen.
:::

```
rtype

dict()

return

Dictionary describing the various image parameters
(size, image format).
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.screen_image_format
documentation:
Retrieves the image format accepted by the screen of the Stream
Deck. Images should be given in this format when drawing on
screen.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.set_screen_image` method to
draw an image on the StreamDeck screen.
:::

```
rtype

dict()

return

Dictionary describing the various image parameters
(size, image format).
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_poll_frequency
documentation:
Sets the frequency of the button polling reader thread, determining how
often the StreamDeck will be polled for button changes.

A higher frequency will result in a higher CPU usage, but a lower
latency between a physical button press and a event from the library.

:param int hz: Reader thread frequency, in Hz (1-1000).

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_key_callback
documentation:
Sets the callback function called each time a button on the StreamDeck
changes state (either pressed, or released).

:::{note}
This callback will be fired from an internal reader thread.
Ensure that the given callback function is thread-safe.
:::

:::{note}
Only one callback can be registered at one time.
:::

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.set_key_callback_async` method for
a version compatible with Python 3 `asyncio` asynchronous
functions.
:::

```
param function callback

Callback function to fire each time a button
state changes.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_key_callback_async
documentation:
Sets the asynchronous callback function called each time a button on the
StreamDeck changes state (either pressed, or released). The given
callback should be compatible with Python 3's `asyncio` routines.

:::{note}
The asynchronous callback will be fired in a thread-safe
manner.
:::

:::{note}
This will override the callback (if any) set by
{func}`~StreamDeck.set_key_callback`.
:::

```
param function async_callback

Asynchronous callback function to fire
each time a button state changes.

param asyncio.loop loop

Asyncio loop to dispatch the callback into
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_dial_callback
documentation:
Sets the callback function called each time there is an interaction
with a dial on the StreamDeck.

:::{note}
This callback will be fired from an internal reader thread.
Ensure that the given callback function is thread-safe.
:::

:::{note}
Only one callback can be registered at one time.
:::

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.set_dial_callback_async` method
for a version compatible with Python 3 `asyncio`
asynchronous functions.
:::

```
param function callback

Callback function to fire each time a button
state changes.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_dial_callback_async
documentation:
Sets the asynchronous callback function called each time there is an
interaction with a dial on the StreamDeck. The given callback should
be compatible with Python 3's `asyncio` routines.

:::{note}
The asynchronous callback will be fired in a thread-safe
manner.
:::

:::{note}
This will override the callback (if any) set by
{func}`~StreamDeck.set_dial_callback`.
:::

```
param function async_callback

Asynchronous callback function to fire
each time a button state changes.

param asyncio.loop loop

Asyncio loop to dispatch the callback into
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_touchscreen_callback
documentation:
Sets the callback function called each time there is an interaction
with a touchscreen on the StreamDeck.

:::{note}
This callback will be fired from an internal reader thread.
Ensure that the given callback function is thread-safe.
:::

:::{note}
Only one callback can be registered at one time.
:::

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.set_touchscreen_callback_async`
method for a version compatible with Python 3 `asyncio`
asynchronous functions.
:::

```
param function callback

Callback function to fire each time a button
state changes.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_touchscreen_callback_async
documentation:
Sets the asynchronous callback function called each time there is an
interaction with the touchscreen on the StreamDeck. The given callback
should be compatible with Python 3's `asyncio` routines.

:::{note}
The asynchronous callback will be fired in a thread-safe
manner.
:::

:::{note}
This will override the callback (if any) set by
{func}`~StreamDeck.set_touchscreen_callback`.
:::

```
param function async_callback

Asynchronous callback function to fire
each time a button state changes.

param asyncio.loop loop

Asyncio loop to dispatch the callback into
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.key_states
documentation:
Retrieves the current states of the buttons on the StreamDeck.

```
rtype

list(bool)

return

List describing the current states of each of the buttons on
the device ( if the button is being pressed, 
otherwise).
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.dial_states
documentation:
Retrieves the current states of the dials (pressed or not) on the
Stream Deck

```
rtype

list(bool)

return

List describing the current states of each of the dials on
the device ( if the dial is being pressed, 
otherwise).
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.reset
documentation:
Resets the StreamDeck, clearing all button images and showing the
standby image.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_brightness
documentation:
Sets the global screen brightness of the StreamDeck, across all the
physical buttons.

:param int/float percent: brightness percent, from [0-100] as an `int`,
                          or normalized to [0.0-1.0] as a `float`.

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.get_serial_number
documentation:
Gets the serial number of the attached StreamDeck.

```
rtype

str

return

String containing the serial number of the attached device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.get_firmware_version
documentation:
Gets the firmware version of the attached StreamDeck.

```
rtype

str

return

String containing the firmware version of the attached device.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_key_image
documentation:
Sets the image of a button on the StreamDeck to the given image. The
image being set should be in the correct format for the device, as an
enumerable collection of bytes.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.key_image_format` method for
information on the image format accepted by the device.
:::

```
param int key

Index of the button whose image is to be updated.

param enumerable image

Raw data of the image to set on the button.
If , the key will be cleared to a black
color.
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_touchscreen_image
documentation:
Draws an image on the touchscreen in a certain position. The image
should be in the correct format for the devices, as an enumerable
collection of bytes.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.touchscreen_image_format` method for
information on the image format accepted by the device.
:::

```
param enumerable image

Raw data of the image to set on the button.
If , the touchscreen will be cleared.

param int x_pos

Position on x axis of the image to draw

param int y_pos

Position on y axis of the image to draw

param int width

width of the image

param int height

height of the image
```

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_key_color
documentation:
Sets the color of the touch buttons. These buttons are indexed
in order after the standard keys.

:param int key: Index of the button
:param int r: Red value
:param int g: Green value
:param int b: Blue value

methods.name: StreamDeck.Devices.StreamDeck.StreamDeck.set_screen_image
documentation:
Draws an image on the touchless screen of the StreamDeck.

:::{seealso}
See {func}`~StreamDeck.screen_image_format` method for
information on the image format accepted by the device.
:::

```
param enumerable image

Raw data of the image to set on the button.
If , the screen will be cleared.
```
Represents a physically attached StreamDeck device.
